subject: The Tale of Two Donnellys
body:
| | |
---
| |
---
|
---
| **_The Tale of Two Donnellys_** What a weekend! Cold weather and snow couldn't put our Republican excitement on ice, with hundreds of grassroots Hoosiers braving the fierce conditions last weekend to join us for our largest Congress of Counties training conference ever. It was great witnessing the excitement we all share for electing Republicans up and down the ballot!

If you were with us at Congress of Counties, then you know that our top priority in 2018 will be defeating Democrat Joe Donnelly and replacing him with a U.S. Senator who will vote to cut taxes, reform health care and grow jobs in Indiana. Hoosiers are tired of hearing one Joe Donnelly in Indiana (who we call Indiana Joe), and a vastly different Donnelly in Washington (otherwise known as D.C. Donnelly), where he consistently votes with Chuck Schumer and his fellow Democrats on the issues that matter most.

And this was yet another week that told the tale of the two Donnellys. Here in Indiana, Democrat Donnelly talks about his support for making sure Hoosier children maintain access to health care coverage through the federal Children's Health Insurance Program. But at the same time it took Democrat Donnelly DAYS to [answer the simple question](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=2ef1acadc9&e=1acd9bb3d6) of whether or not he would support a bill that does just that -- and prevents a federal government shutdown. In fact, he was so unwilling to answer the question that when asked by reporters he told them to "just give my office a call."

That won't cut it...both Joe Donnellys have got to go.

-Kyle Hupfer

_Picture: Chairman Kyle Hupfer takes a photo with a team of 8th District Republicans at last weekend's Congress of Counties training conference._
---
| |
---
| | **_Governor Holcomb's Week That Was_**
---
|
---
| This week delivered lots of great news for Indiana, and Governor Eric Holcomb was on the road to join Hoosiers to celebrate. This includes:
* Indiana received big new yesterday, when **Amazon announced that the state is a finalist for its new HQ2**! A win like this could bring $5 billion in investment and 50,000 new, high-wage jobs to Indiana!
* **South Bend International Airport has landed twice daily flights to Charlotte and Dallas/Fort Worth** , better connecting Hoosiers with destinations across the nation and around the world!
* And, significant here on the political side, news broke this week that **Governor Holcomb set a record for campaign cash on hand in 2017**! His campaign closed 2017 with $1.71 million, setting a new record for funds in the bank for a gubernatorial campaign at the end of a governor’s first year in office. This record cash-on-hand position shows that __Hoosiers across Indiana are excited to show their support for Governor Holcomb’s transformative, solutions-based approach to governing__!

---

|

| **Supporting Life at the National March for Life** Thousands of Americans from across the country gathered today in Washington, D.C. to show the groundswell of support for our fundamental right to life!

President Trump and Vice President Pence both addressed the massive crowd. That's while every Republican member of the U.S. House of Representatives, including all of our Hoosiers representatives, voted in favor of the [Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=b5041c15cf&e=1acd9bb3d6), which establishes criminal penalties to punish abortion providers who fail to give medical attention and care to babies born after a failed attempt at an abortion.

If you couldn't catch today's big rally in Washington, consider joining Monday's Indiana March for Life in Indianapolis! [Get full details here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=034965ce59&e=1acd9bb3d6).

name: DNC Rapid Response
subject: Trump shutdown → Here's what's next:
body:
This is a critical moment to show you're standing with Democrats.
---
It's official, Derek -- Republicans have shut down the federal government.

They control the White House and both chambers of Congress, and under their watch, they haven't been able to govern and keep the lights on.

If they're not capable of doing the job they were elected to do, we know plenty of Democrats running across the country who are up to the task.

**[Pitch in $10 right now to throw Republicans out and elect Democrats who will get the job done.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/405223bb/4067b0a/4b40b9baf/7e510abd/3818288649/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/govt-shutdown?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+Pitch+in+10+right+now+to+throw+Republica&utm_campaign=em_20180120_dnc_nd&source=em_20180120_dnc_nd&refcode=em_20180120_dnc_nd")**

With your help, we'll make Republicans pay for this.

name: Nina Turner, Our Revolution
subject: The government shutdown
body:
A government shutdown over Congress' inability to compromise
#
Friends,
A government shutdown over Congress' inability to compromise is disgraceful, yet unsurprising. The Republicans have shown their cowardice when it comes to passing a clean DREAM Act. Instead of fighting for the 800,000 Dreamers that need permanent status in this country, they are more concerned with pleasing President Trump. Republicans are choosing to serve the president, not the American people.
A government shutdown is a failure of leadership. Approximately 850,000 federal workers will be furloughed including food safety workers, passport office employees, and IRS call center staff. Republicans in Congress would rather cause mass dysfunction than work to fix our broken immigration system.
[**Chip in $5 to Our Revolution's organizing efforts right now and help us emerge from this government shutdown ready to take our government back from the special interests.**](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad55/bed43eb/3321e9d7/75feb117/600024901/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ourrev-2018?refcode=em180120-suba&noskip=true")
In the absence of leadership from our government, we the people must step up. name: Tom Perez
subject: 1 year of Trump → 1 day of shutdown
body:
The next step to defeating Trump and his Republican Party is winning in 2018.
---
On Trump's one-year anniversary as president, we're on day one of a government shutdown.

Make no mistake, Derek. This shutdown happened because this president and his party are too incompetent and ill-intentioned to do their jobs and keep the federal government up and running. It happened because they are incapable of governing, and it's time for it to end.

Here's the good news: We can take back the House AND the Senate from them this November.

Whether we win or lose this year depends on the work we put in right now. And there's no better way to commemorate Trump's first year in office than by showing him we're ready to elect Democrats who will stand up to his awful agenda.

**Contribute $3 to invest in Democrats across the country. If we work together starting right now, we can take back Congress this year.**

[DONATE: $3](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/405223ca/4067b0a/4b32abb97/7e51e16c/2928152023/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/1-year-trump?amount=3&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE+3&utm_campaign=em_20180120_tp_nd&source=em_20180120_tp_nd&refcode=em_20180120_tp_nd")

name: Team Kaine
subject: It's time for a reckoning
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
Today, it's been exactly one year since Donald Trump's inauguration. For someone who, on almost all fronts, seems to prefer behaving as a political talk-show host to being the actual President, we shouldn't be surprised (though it is disappointing) that we're marking this anniversary with the government shut down.

**Take away the spin and the pundits, and the reality in Washington, D.C. is this: The GOP controls the federal government. Donald Trump and congressional Republicans had a chance to negotiate a bipartisan deal to keep the government open. They refused.
**
This shutdown comes at the end of what has been an almost unthinkably bad first year in office for Donald Trump. Each day seems to bring a new controversy or outrage, and it all follows this basic structure:

1\. Trump says or does something bad (likely on Twitter).
2\. Americans are disgusted and angry.
3\. Americans speak up.
4\. Americans are forced to move on because Trump has done the next outrageous thing.

And so the cycle continues, and each outrage blurs with the one before until everyone can feel too exhausted to even move. But anniversaries are a time for reflection, so today we're looking back at the "greatest hits" of each month of Trump's presidency to remind ourselves what we're fighting against when we roll up our sleeves to elect (or re-elect) progressive leaders in Congress. **This is a long email, but it's time for a reckoning, so read on if you're ready for one:**

**January 2017** : Trump signs his first travel ban, effectively blocking people from Muslim countries, including Syrian refugees, from entering the United States.

**February 2017** : Trump reverses guidelines put in place by the Obama Administration that allow transgender students in public schools to choose which bathroom to use.

**March 2017** : It's reported that the FBI has information suggesting possible coordination between the Trump campaign and Russia in 2016, prompting one of Trump's all-too-frequent "fake news" tweets.

**April 2017** : Trump takes some time away from his busy schedule watching cable news to have dinner *in the White House* with pals Sarah Palin, Kid Rock, and Ted Nugent (whose past comments about Barack Obama and Hillary Clinton, among others, are so offensive we can't even type them here).

**May 2017** : Trump fires FBI Director James Comey, telling Lester Holt it was because of "this Russia thing." Soon after, Robert Mueller is appointed as special counsel to investigate Russian meddling in the election.

**June 2017** : Trump announces his plans to withdraw from the Paris climate deal.

**July 2017** : With urging from Trump, congressional Republicans come dangerously close to repealing the Affordable Care Act. The repeal is prevented by just one vote in the Senate.

**August 2017** : After white supremacists descend on Charlottesville with hatred and violence, Trump reacts to the tragic events by noting that there were "very fine people on both sides" of the issue.

**September 2017** : Trump directly attacks NFL players who chose to peacefully protest racial violence, calling for them to be fired.

**October 2017** : Republicans Bob Corker and Jeff Flake, fed up with a lack of responsible governing, voice their frustrations with Trump. Neither plans to return to the Senate after their terms end.

**November 2017** : Trump defends Alabama Senate candidate Roy Moore despite multiple credible accusations of sexual abuse of underage girls.

**December 2017** : Trump signs a deficit-busting 1.5 trillion dollar tax cut into law benefitting corporations and the wealthiest in the country, and leaving the middle class and working people holding the bag.

**January 2018** : Feeling defensive -- and as he taunts North Korea over the prospect of a nuclear strike -- Trump tweets out a reminder to his 46 million followers that he is a "stable genius."

Well, there you have it. One year in office. And this, of course, doesn't even come close to covering all that's happened. The cruel reversal of DACA. Ending protections for workers. Rolling back Wall Street reform. Appointing the anti-public education Betsy DeVos and anti-civil rights Jeff Sessions to his cabinet. Countless insults and untruths.

If you made it this far down, you're probably feeling a little frustrated and dispirited. We get it. But here's the thing: There may be three more years in Trump's term, but we have a chance this fall to earn a Democratic majority in the Senate, which would give us a progressive brick wall against the dangerous and divisive agenda being pushed by this administration. It would create a real check to this presidency that we so desperately need.

**Ready?[Add your name if you're mad that this is our reality. Add your name if you're ready to fight for change. Add your name if you're ready for Democrats to win big in 2018.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=2yNwvOfg0t34m7Yfp4PFQlckOvvk%2BJL9)
**
Thank you for all you do. It's not easy, and we're with you every step of the way.

In solidarity,

Team Kaine

May 2017 : Trump fires FBI Director James Comey, telling Lester Holt it was because of "this Russia thing." Soon after, Robert Mueller is appointed as special counsel to investigate Russian meddling in the election. June 2017 : Trump announces his plans to withdraw from the Paris climate deal. July 2017 : With urging from Trump, congressional Republicans come dangerously close to repealing the Affordable Care Act. The repeal is prevented by just one vote in the Senate. August 2017 : After white supremacists descend on Charlottesville with hatred and violence, Trump reacts to the tragic events by noting that there were "very fine people on both sides" of the issue. September 2017 : Trump directly attacks NFL players who chose to peacefully protest racial violence, calling for them to be fired. October 2017 : Republicans Bob Corker and Jeff Flake, fed up with a lack of responsible governing, voice their frustrations with Trump. Neither plans to return to the Senate after their terms end. November 2017 : Trump defends Alabama Senate candidate Roy Moore despite multiple credible accusations of sexual abuse of underage girls. December 2017 : Trump signs a deficit-busting 1.5 trillion dollar tax cut into law benefitting corporations and the wealthiest in the country, and leaving the middle class and working people holding the bag. January 2018 : Feeling defensive -- and as he taunts North Korea over the prospect of a nuclear strike -- Trump tweets out a reminder to his 46 million followers that he is a "stable genius." Well, there you have it. One year in office. And this, of course, doesn't even come close to covering all that's happened. The cruel reversal of DACA. Ending protections for workers. Rolling back Wall Street reform. Appointing the anti-public education Betsy DeVos and anti-civil rights Jeff Sessions to his cabinet. Countless insults and untruths. If you made it this far down, you're probably feeling a little frustrated and dispirited. We get it. But here's the thing: There may be three more years in Trump's term, but we have a chance this fall to earn a Democratic majority in the Senate, which would give us a progressive brick wall against the dangerous and divisive agenda being pushed by this administration. It would create a real check to this presidency that we so desperately need. Ready? Thank you for all you do. It's not easy, and we're with you every step of the way. In solidarity, Team Kaine | | Paid for by Paid for by Kaine for Virginia | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.

name: Stephanie Schriock, EMILY's List
subject: [Women's March] ① year later
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6b18/24c69fd9/132f87e08/1895fcb9/13497021/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEM36N_digital_email_20180120_WomensMarchAnniversary_inact&source=WQEFEM36N_digital_email_20180120_WomensMarchAnniversary_inact")
---

Peter,

"We've got two choices... We either run for office, or we support a sister who is running for office."

That was my challenge on stage one year ago, on a cold winter day in D.C., to hundreds of thousands gathered for the Women's March and the millions more watching online and participating around the world.

It's been 12 months, and over 26,000 women across the country in _every single state_ have accepted the call to run.

Last year wasn't just the start of a wave. It was the beginning of a sea change that could elect thousands of women over the _next decade_.

**[Donate $3 to keep the spirit of the Women's March going by supporting our brave pro-choice Democratic women running for office this year and for years to come.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6b18/24c69fd9/132f87e08/1895fcba/13497021/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180121_email_WomensMarchAnniversary?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+keep+the+spirit+of+the+Women&utm_campaign=WQEFEM36N_digital_email_20180120_WomensMarchAnniversary_inact&source=WQEFEM36N_digital_email_20180120_WomensMarchAnniversary_inact")**

I walked off the stage last year feeling hopeful for the first time in months.

And that hope grew the next day when a record 500 women joined us for a training on how to get ready to run for office.

And this community -- we just kept going. In the last year, we've trained over 2,500 women on how to run for office. We racked up historic wins in Virginia, Georgia, and Washington State. Already this year, we helped Patty Schachtner rack up a _10-point win in a Wisconsin state Senate district that went for Trump by 17 points_.

Hope is everywhere: in every woman who tells us she wants to run, every candidate we endorse, every person who volunteers to knock doors or call voters, and in the millions of people in the EMILY's List community who keep fighting every single day.

There is a brighter future on the horizon for us -- if we dare to claim it.

**Donate $3 to continue electing pro-choice Democratic women and launch a sea change that transforms our politics for a generation:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Keep-the-Womens-March-Going](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6b18/24c69fd9/132f87e08/1895fcbb/13497021/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180121_email_WomensMarchAnniversary?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgKeeptheWomensMar&utm_campaign=WQEFEM36N_digital_email_20180120_WomensMarchAnniversary_inact&source=WQEFEM36N_digital_email_20180120_WomensMarchAnniversary_inact")**

Thanks for all you do,

Stephanie Schriock
President, EMILY's List

Team,

It's official: Republicans just shut down the federal government.

This is the first shutdown in history that's happened when _one_ party controls the White House, the Senate and the House.

And why did it happen? Because Trump, McConnell, and Ryan would rather shut down the government, causing chaos and hardship for working Americans, than work with Democrats on the bipartisan deal that was already on the table.

[**It 's reckless, irresponsible and it has to end now. Add your name: tell Republicans to work with Democrats on a bipartisan deal and reopen the government immediately.**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d64d/2b07114c/be7d499d/731b9e13/3364446705/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/end-trump-shutdown?source=PT-20180120-Breaking_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

body:

Friends,
Wanted to be sure that you saw this email!
**We're stamping out Citizens United in your neighborhood. Can you attend an event tomorrow?**
****[Find an event](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad5e/bed43eb/327d035e/75feb305/1924376107/VEsH/ "https://actionnetwork.org/event_campaigns/j21-stamp-money-out-athon-parties")****
In Solidarity,
The Team at Our Revolution
\---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Our Revolution
Date: Wed, Jan 17, 2018 at 4:13 PM
Subject: Fwd: Join a stamp-a-thon event near you!@ourrevolution.com>
Friends,
Next Sunday is the 8th anniversary of the Citizens United decision that allowed unlimited campaign money to buy access and influence with local, state, and federal officials. We are teaming up with Ben Cohen (co-founder of Ben & Jerry's Ice Cream), Stamp Stampede, and other partners to harness the power of people over dollars.
**Can you join us this week through Sunday, Jan 21, 2018, as we legally rubber stamp messages like "Not To Be Used To Bribe Politicians" on over 1 million bills?**
****[Find an event](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad5e/bed43eb/327d035e/75feb305/1924376107/VEsE/ "https://actionnetwork.org/event_campaigns/j21-stamp-money-out-athon-parties")****
Come together with friends, family, and neighbors across the country on Sunday, January 21 at 4 p.m. to legally rubber stamp bills and tune in for a special message from Ben Cohen, Jeff Clements, and other national leaders.
For updates on the campaign and a reminder to tune in to the livestream, [click here!](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ad5e/bed43eb/327d035e/75feb304/1924376107/VEsF/ "https://www.stampstampede.org/pledge/")

name: Barbara Mikulski
subject: Friend, you helped spark a movement. Will you keep it going?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
It's been a year since since the Women's March, when women across the U.S. came together to march the streets of their cities and towns with one purpose: to resist President Trump's misogynist, sexist agenda.
---
[](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018a?refcode=DPE-20180120-Mikulski-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
Friend --

It's been a year since since the Women's March, when women across the U.S. came together to march the streets of their cities and towns with one purpose: to resist President Trump's misogynist, sexist agenda.

This sparked a movement of resistance, that up to this day, refuses to die down -- a movement that continues to grow exponentially. And for that, I'm filled with much hope.

I'm hopeful that more women will continue to raise their voices and be heard. Because if there's one thing that I learned during my time in the Senate is that when women use their voices loudly, they're heard, and that in turn, shifts the national debate to reflect the needs of American families.

[**That 's why I'm committed to helping our 11 female Democratic Senators get re-elected this year. Will you join me in helping our female Democratic Senate candidates? Simply chip in $1 to the DSCC Women's Senate Network and help ensure our candidates have strong, tough campaigns to win.**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018a?refcode=DPE-20180120-Mikulski-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

We also have a great chance of adding two more women to the Senate and flipping seats from red to blue in Nevada and Arizona with Jacky Rosen and Kyrsten Sinema.

Look, friend, our female Democratic Senators know how to fight the good fight -- from championing policies on equal pay for equal work to affordable healthcare -- and you bet they'll continue to raise their voices loudly to fight for us if we keep them in the Senate.

[**So friend, it 's our turn to use our voice and help fight for our female candidates. Chip in to show your support by giving to the DSCC Women's Senate Network today.**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/wsn-2018a?refcode=DPE-20180120-Mikulski-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

Let's continue to encourage and support our women to raise their voices -- in the U.S. Senate and beyond.

Thank you,

Barbara Mikulski
Former U.S. Senator

name: Van Hollen HQ
subject: last night
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __
Last night, Republican leaders chose to shut down our government. It's a tragedy and an outrage.

Despite the fact that many Democrats and Republicans agree on the outlines of a deal to end this standoff, President Trump and Republican leaders chose brinkmanship and political posturing over finding common solutions.

[**Make no mistake: Republicans control the House, the Senate and the White House -- the entire decision-making apparatus in Washington -- and those leaders have made it crystal clear that they are unwilling to come together and compromise to keep our government open.**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91860/70cdc2ba/46f4da6c/3903f4f/1142889379/VEsH/ "https://cvanhollen.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/republican-shutdown?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Make+no+mistake+Republicans+control+the+&utm_campaign=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_180120&source=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_180120")

Instead, Speaker Ryan, Majority Leader McConnell and President Trump are trying to pull the wool over the eyes of the country by tweeting blame on Democrats. But the American people are smarter than that. They know Republicans are in charge and could reverse this situation in a minute if they would stop playing politics and just do their jobs.

We need to extend the Children's Health Insurance Program; fund disaster recovery for the tragedies that have rocked our communities in Texas, Florida, Puerto Rico, and the Virgin Islands; protect 800,000 DACA recipients; and stop governing by short-term funding gimmicks that hurt our national defense, our veterans, and our work to fight the opioid epidemic and help our economy.

Instead of dealing with these priorities, Republican leaders want to kick the can down the road yet again.

[**It 's time to stop putting our national interests at risk. Will you stand with Chris to fight this outrage?**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91860/70cdc2ba/46f4da6c/3903f4c/1142889379/VEsE/ "https://cvanhollen.cp.bsd.net/page/signup/republican-shutdown?utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+Itrsquos+time+to+stop+putting+our+nation&utm_campaign=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_180120&source=CVH_EM_PET_FULL_180120")

Team Van Hollen

Thanks to Senator Baldwin, last night, the government shut down. Because she wants to play politics, Tammy is putting pay for our troops and funding
*|MC_PREVIEW_TEXT|* | | |

| | |

| | | Jan. 20, 2018
---
| |

**Thanks to Senator Baldwin, last night, the government shut down.** Because she wants to play politics, Tammy is putting pay for our troops and funding for children's health care in jeopardy. Look at what she tweeted back in 2013 about a government shutdown... This is hypocrisy at its finest.
---
| As Wisconsinites went to D.C. on Friday to the March for Life, Leah also wrote [an open letter to Senator Baldwin](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=34def1abd5e54576ba6335870ed3a1a7&_e=mevL2dRXiWkPSkm3cBtLqkV_u9NU0U_QL7GBd8lnu8q_ND2V0VknUEircmwlYXENMDZ4VBlJIuuoSLOQFsjDPmKx3AZzGc7MY3qBovK_Njscd_oxaasX147030s5wmKwt0ZWg4bFyCmHCA0cPR4Gp9GuI57Ecdgef4i4m10EeyHiLIAwTBD9oXqk8c3mVbUyQe32E79US8FTmN0IGGL8gG8NALKbdjmgpIPKHWC12rJqlNHnk4Eyl2VtHnV5bMR2Jgmn4kX8Jvehvzz0_iwZvpu95UNGStWKGcNcqkIN-TEgFOzDpWrE-W3SlII6CEPk) on how Tammy doesn't listen to her pro-life constituents.
---
| | [ ](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=34def1abd5e54576ba6335870ed3a1a7&_e=mevL2dRXiWkPSkm3cBtLqgaga_AaF5tXPCWn4VwANEOmL-TMLzfeLEs1aMrX9H4bRvYqfaA-DFoq8YDscc4XbUMdHNruF7lEeyVV45O2D6KW4VE48ep5eOJ__hKd3h8S-7M5m4l2E9SdrvClD0mDj4LZ-i2OOpDujd6jROwdeUmRDAGensytReNTLSogknl8Mrz0gCEesGvoFHbbitYRVmY3_rkZBqBWj2YTdz-De0nVsc6S5l_hAcDmC2aJwXGqZuZzp19iYARvqz2JGG2vsYqCgeC3BONjjl9s6-Uol-o%3D)
---
Unlike in Washington, we have some great news here in Wisconsin...

**Wisconsin's unemployment rate hit 3.0%** — the lowest the state has ever seen.

This was only able to happen because of Leah's work with Governor Walker and her colleagues in the Legislature to make Wisconsin open for business!
---
| Leah's been busy traveling across Wisconsin, hearing about how Tammy Baldwin isn't working for Wisconsinites. She has driven more than 40,000 miles talking to voters since she started considering running for U.S. Senate.
---
|
---
| This week, Leah was in Shawano, Pepin, Oconto, Winnebago, Crawford, Milwaukee, Ozaukee and Brown Counties. Today she's attending Republican Party caucuses in La Crosse and Trempeleau Counties.
---
| |
---
Sen. Patrick Testin from Stevens Point [wrote an op-ed](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=34def1abd5e54576ba6335870ed3a1a7&_e=9PrSxczjYpP2s2JJVbDb5sUhJbRq8nSbvq5c6Sk3jPHmnSFdmhJmGJdH-XvKOlq8RViCNhtjJMfknlMcQHlgP0KdcvkiUuIS8scLnFt5TJuv01FLYOdNkoSO0GI9aLAcUQ7zSc2whyXLKuUFwtR_hiQyLXwnklpFh8B-KSGfzKfBbg82FWDZUKywwPVy3UpgYsmxG34wyPEoCpGiPq_5lJ42ehQinC9DUxPQdtWJCSyEVsYKsZsnSWIZ1SKTqTFyx87YO_Ve8lnn57ft93tnwT4aJW0fkrtWp1clTb_4FYyReVTPFYSwtSPVKZvRjm94e6_zfH6mPo4fuoOIkTOIhvf7Lg7qK7STXpECgxIxAL8Vy_vxWWuReKOG0RWQy2-nUkNTipZ8MjEA64bo8xNussbeldJKwWUaT6kw5uNTxmE%3D) in the Tomah Journal about why he is supporting Leah for U.S. Senate.

"There is no doubt in my mind that D.C. could learn a thing or two from what we have been able to accomplish here in Wisconsin, and that is why I am proudly supporting Leah and ask that you give her your vote."
---

The #TrumpShutdown has begun -- and according to the _Washington Post_ , Trump's throwing a party at Mar-A-Lago, in his honor! Let's show the GOP that we aren't taking this lying down and that we are fighting back.
---
[](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d651/2b07114c/bf166ab0/731b84a8/4105459223/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/end-the-trump-shutdown?source=PT-20180120-AddYourNameDS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")
_**Washington Post: "Trump plans gala at Mar-a-Lago tonight...
renting a ballroom from himself"**_
Team,

The #TrumpShutdown has begun -- and Donald Trump is throwing a party at Mar-A-Lago.

That's right, while thousands upon thousands of people (including our military) don't know when they'll get paid again, Donald Trump is throwing a gala _in his honor_ at Mar-a-Lago tonight.

[**We need to fight back, team. Will you add your name to show that grassroots Democrats are fired up and more determined than ever to kick the GOP to the curb this November? Add your name now >>**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d651/2b07114c/bf166ab0/731b84a8/4105459223/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/end-the-trump-shutdown?source=PT-20180120-AddYourNameDS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

And why does Trump think he can get away with his party? Because the GOP spin machine is in overdrive.

We know the GOP "leadership" ignored a bipartisan deal to keep the government open, waited for MONTHS to address the fact that they let CHIP expire (while they took care of tax cuts for billionaires), and now they're on every talk show trying to convince the American public that the blame for this shutdown doesn't lie with them?? The party that controls the White House, the Senate, and the House? It's infuriating!

**Let 's show the GOP that grassroots Democrats aren't just mad -- we are taking action to kick them out. Add your name to say we can count on you to fight back:**
